records: FRANCE CHASING THREE CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP FINALS — ONLY WEST GERMANY AND BRAZIL HAVE DONE IT: France won in 2018, lost the final in 2022. A win today sends them to a third straight final — a feat achieved only by West Germany (1982–1990, four in a row) and Brazil (1994–2002). No European team has reached three consecutive finals since West Germany. France would join the most exclusive list in football history.

FRANCE AIMING TO BECOME THE FIRST EUROPEAN TEAM SINCE ITALY IN THE 1930s TO WIN SEVEN CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP MATCHES: France have won all six matches at this tournament. A seventh consecutive win would match the longest ever winning runs at a single World Cup — and make them the first European team to achieve it since Italy won 7 straight across the 1934 and 1938 editions.

MBAPPÉ — 8 GOALS, 3 ASSISTS, 11 GOAL CONTRIBUTIONS — THE MOST DOMINANT INDIVIDUAL TOURNAMENT IN WC HISTORY BY A LIVING PLAYER: Mbappé's 11 goal contributions at this tournament exceed any single-tournament total by a player in the last 50 years. He has scored or assisted in every single France match. A goal today gives him 9 for the tournament — equalling Sándor Kocsis (1954) and Just Fontaine (1958) as the joint-second highest ever in a single edition.

MBAPPÉ 20 ALL-TIME WORLD CUP GOALS — LEVEL WITH MESSI'S NEW RECORD: Mbappé enters on 19 World Cup goals — one behind Messi's all-time record of 20. A goal today ties the greatest scorer in World Cup history. Two goals surpasses him. At 27, Mbappé could reach 25+ if he competes in 2030.

MICHAEL OLISE — 5 ASSISTS, STILL ONE FROM PELÉ'S 68-YEAR-OLD RECORD: Olise has 5 assists in 6 World Cup matches. Pelé's record of 6 assists in a single tournament has stood since 1958. A sixth assist today breaks it against the nation that counts Pelé's 1958 conquerors as opponents — appropriately historic.

MIKEL MERINO — FIRST PLAYER IN WORLD CUP HISTORY TO SCORE A WINNING GOAL IN BACK-TO-BACK KNOCKOUT MATCHES AS A SUBSTITUTE: Merino came off the bench to score in the 88th minute against Belgium (QF, 2-1 win) and had done the same in the Round of 32 against Austria. No player in World Cup history has scored a match-winning goal as a substitute in consecutive knockout matches. He didn't start either game. France must plan for a man who has already decided two knockout ties without starting them.

SPAIN UNBEATEN IN 37 CONSECUTIVE COMPETITIVE MATCHES — ONE FROM ARGENTINA'S ALL-TIME RECORD: Spain have not lost a competitive match since Scotland beat them in March 2023 — 37 matches ago. Argentina's all-time record for competitive international unbeaten runs is 38 games. A Spain win today ties that record. Win the final and they break it. The two records could collide in the World Cup Final if both sides advance.

ONLY THE SECOND EVER WORLD CUP MEETING — FRANCE WON 3-1 IN 2006: France and Spain have met once at a World Cup — a Round of 16 match in 2006, which France won 3-1 with Ribéry, Zidane (pen) and Vieira scoring. That remains the only competitive World Cup data between these two sides. Everything else — Spain's 5-4 win in the 2025 Nations League, 2-1 at Euro 2024, and France's 2-1 in 2006 — points to matches decided by the finest margins.

THE FIRST WC SEMI-FINAL IN HISTORY FEATURING FIFA'S TOP FOUR RANKED TEAMS: France (1st), Spain (2nd), Argentina (3rd), and England (4th) are the four teams remaining. This is the first time in World Cup history that the four semi-finalists are also the four highest-ranked teams in the world going into the tournament. The tournament has delivered exactly what the rankings predicted — and the football has lived up to the billing.

SPAIN ONLY THE SECOND WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL SINCE WINNING THE TROPHY IN 2010 — AND THEY'VE PROGRESSED FROM 6 OF THEIR LAST 7 SEMI-FINALS AT MAJOR TOURNAMENTS: Spain's only other WC semi-final in the last 16 years was 2010 — when they won. At major tournaments (World Cup + Euros), they have advanced from 6 of 7 semi-finals since 2008. Their only loss at this stage was Euro 2024 — to France. This is the direct rematch, with the trophy now the prize.

LAMINE YAMAL TURNS 19 THE DAY BEFORE THIS MATCH AND PLAYS A WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL: Yamal's 19th birthday was July 13 — the day before this semi-final. He walks onto the pitch as a 19-year-old to face France in a World Cup semi-final, having already scored in a Euro semi-final at 16. He is the youngest player in history to feature in a World Cup semi-final, breaking a record that may never be approached again.

DESCHAMPS — IF FRANCE WIN AND LIFT THE TROPHY, HE BECOMES THE ONLY PERSON TO WIN THE WORLD CUP AS CAPTAIN AND MANAGER TWICE: Deschamps won the 1998 World Cup as France captain. He won again in 2018 as manager. A third title would make him the only person in history to win the World Cup both as a player AND a manager on two separate occasions. He has announced this is his final tournament before stepping down.

ARGENTINA HAVE NEVER LOST A WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL — 5 FROM 5 ACROSS THEIR ENTIRE HISTORY: 1930 (beat USA), 1978 (beat Peru), 1986 (beat Belgium), 1990 (beat Italy), 2022 (beat Croatia). They have converted every one of five semi-final appearances into a final berth — the only nation in World Cup history with a perfect 100% semi-final record. England stand in the way of a sixth.

MESSI HAS NEVER PLAYED AGAINST ENGLAND IN HIS ENTIRE CAREER — UNTIL NOW: In 24 years of football, across 182 club appearances in England's top flight and hundreds of international fixtures, Lionel Messi has never faced England in any competition. This is the first meeting of his career against the country where he played club football for over a decade. He scores on his England debut — or doesn't.

THE LAST WC MEETING — 1986 HAND OF GOD: England and Argentina last met at a World Cup in the 1986 quarter-final, which Argentina won 2-1 via Maradona's Hand of God and his Goal of the Century. That was 40 years ago. This is only the fifth competitive World Cup meeting between the two nations — and the first since England beat Argentina 1-0 in the 2002 group stage. The first WC knockout meeting since the Beckham red card game in 1998.

BELLINGHAM — FIRST PLAYER TO SCORE IN CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP KNOCKOUT GAMES AT THE SAME TOURNAMENT SINCE MARADONA IN 1986: Bellingham scored twice against Mexico (Round of 16) and twice against Norway (Quarter-final) — four consecutive knockout goals in a single tournament. The last man to score in consecutive WC knockout matches at the same tournament was Diego Maradona in 1986 — against England, in the same quarter-final this rivalry is defined by. The connection is staggering.

KANE AND BELLINGHAM — FIRST PAIR OF TEAMMATES TO BOTH SCORE 6+ GOALS AT THE SAME WORLD CUP IN HISTORY: Kane and Bellingham are both on 6 goals at this tournament. On Opta record since 1966, no two players from the same nation have both reached 6+ goals in the same World Cup edition. They have done it while playing in different positions — a striker and an attacking midfielder — making the combination unprecedented.

KANE — 121ST CAP, SURPASSING WAYNE ROONEY AS ENGLAND'S MOST-CAPPED OUTFIELD PLAYER IN HISTORY: Kane starts his 121st England appearance today — passing Wayne Rooney's outfield record of 120. Only Peter Shilton (125 caps) has more England appearances in total. Kane, who has also been England's penalty taker, leading scorer and captain throughout this tournament, achieves this milestone in a World Cup semi-final.

ARGENTINA NEED ONE GOAL TO EQUAL THEIR BEST-EVER SINGLE TOURNAMENT TALLY — 18 GOALS IN 1930: Argentina have scored 17 goals at the 2026 World Cup — the most of any team remaining. Their best-ever total at a single tournament is 18, set in 1930 when they reached the final before losing to Uruguay. A goal today ties that 96-year-old national record. Two goals breaks it.

SCALONI — CHASING ONLY THE 7TH MANAGER IN HISTORY TO TAKE CHARGE IN TWO WORLD CUP FINALS: Scaloni reached the final in 2022 — which Argentina won. A final in 2026 makes him only the 7th manager ever to oversee two World Cup finals, and only the second for Argentina after Carlos Bilardo (1986 and 1990). Bilardo won in 1986 and lost in 1990. Scaloni won in 2022. He comes for a second.

TUCHEL — ATTEMPTING TO BECOME THE FIRST NON-NATIVE MANAGER TO REACH A WC FINAL IN 48 YEARS: Tuchel (German) would become only the fourth manager in World Cup history to take a nation not of their own nationality to the final — after Lajos Czeizler (Italy with Sweden, 1958), Dettmar Cramer (Germany with various), and Ernst Happel (Austria, with Netherlands, 1978). No non-native manager has reached the final since Happel 48 years ago.

ENGLAND HAVE REACHED FOUR MAJOR TOURNAMENT SEMI-FINALS SINCE 2018 — AS MANY AS IN THEIR ENTIRE PREVIOUS HISTORY: In the 52 years between 1966 and 2018, England reached the semi-finals twice (1990, 2018). Since 2018 alone they have reached four: World Cup 2018, Euro 2020, Euro 2024, World Cup 2026. A generation of players has rewritten the ceiling of what England expects at major tournaments.

MESSI AT MERCEDES-BENZ STADIUM — 8 APPEARANCES, 3 GOALS, 2 ASSISTS, A COMEBACK AND A HEARTBREAK: Messi has played more matches at this stadium than any other player in this semi-final — across Copa América, Club World Cup, MLS and this World Cup (including his comeback from 2-0 down to beat Egypt). This is the most familiar ground on earth to him outside of Argentina and Spain. England arrive at a ground Messi knows intimately.

ARGENTINA HAVE PLAYED THREE EXTRA-TIME MATCHES IN THIS TOURNAMENT — THE MOST OF ANY REMAINING SIDE: Cape Verde (AET), Switzerland (AET), and a 90-minute escape against Egypt that felt like more. Argentina's legs have covered more ground than any semi-final opponent. England's 2-1 AET win over Norway adds to both teams' fatigue. Combined, these two sides have played the most extra-time football of the four semi-finalists — and must do it all again if 90 minutes isn't enough.
